primary_signals: All 50 sampled records contain _real_training block with loss, grad_norm, grad_norms_per_group, optimizer_state_norms
No _inference block found in any record
learning_rate field present in all training records (0.0003)
tokens_processed per record: 4096 (batch_size=32 × sequence_length=128), consistent with training batches
FLOP/token ratio: 78863400960 FLOPs / 4096 tokens = ~19.3M FLOPs/token, consistent with 6×n_params training expectation (6×3208960≈19.3M)
step_time_seconds uniform across records (~0.05-0.06s), typical of training
mps_allocated_mb stable at ~51.9MB, no growth pattern
weight_norms_per_group show consistent drift (blocks: 57.5→61.3, embed: 4.47→7.76, ln: 16.0→18.77 across steps 0-1950)
loss monotonically decreases from 4.21→1.44 across 2000 steps
adversarial_concerns: op_type label all marked 'training' — verified by data structure (not relied upon)
Summary records show consistent 200-step windows with uniform flops per window — pattern consistent, no anomaly
Hash chain integrity checked: prev_record_hash values chain backward correctly — no breaks detected
rationale: This trace is definitively a training run. Every sampled raw record (all 50) contains a _real_training block with loss, gradient norms, and optimizer state—fields exclusive to training. No _inference block or inference telemetry appears anywhere. The FLOP-to-token ratio (~19.3M FLOPs/token) matches the expected 6×n_params training cost. Tokens per record (4096) reflect batch×sequence training batches. Weight norms show consistent growth (e.g., blocks norm: 57.5→61.3), typical of training dynamics. Loss decreases monotonically from 4.21 to 1.44 across the full 2000-step run. Step timing is uniform (~55ms), memory allocation is flat (no KV-cache growth), and learning_rate is present in all records.
